pages:
Verdant Health Advisory LLCwww.verdanthealthadvisory.com
May 31, 2021
Laura Phillips
Chief Financial Officer
Johnson, Cross and Gibbs
Dear Ms. Phillips:
This letter sets out the terms on which our firm will conduct a review of physician
compensation arrangements for Johnson, Cross and Gibbs. It follows our discussion
of the past several weeks and replaces the outline I left with you at that meeting. If
the terms below match your understanding, please sign where indicated and return
a copy to me. We are prepared to begin on June 10, 2021.
I want to be plain at the outset about what this engagement is and is not. You have
asked whether the compensation arrangements you have in place are defensible on
a fair-market-value basis. We will tell you what our analysis supports. Where the
record is thin, we will say that the record is thin rather than fill the gap with an
inference, and where a conclusion is qualified we will state the qualification in the
same sentence as the conclusion. I mention this because engagement letters often
promise comfort. We do not promise comfort. We promise work you can hand to
counsel without apologizing for it.
Scope of Work
We will review the physician compensation arrangements you identify to us at
kickoff, together with the underlying employment and professional services
agreements, the productivity and quality data used to calculate compensation under
those agreements, and the documentation supporting how each arrangement was
approved. Our review will address whether the compensation payable under each
arrangement is consistent with fair market value and commercially reasonable, and
whether the arrangement as administered matches the arrangement as written.
That last point is frequently where the difficulty sits.
Our work will not constitute a legal opinion, an audit, an examination, or a review
conducted in accordance with attestation standards, and it should not be relied
upon as any of those. We are not counsel and will not opine on the application of any
Verdant Health Advisory LLC
Page 1 of 4

statute or regulation to your arrangements. We will not perform procedures to
detect fraud. Our conclusions will rest on the documents and data you provide and
on the representations of the people we interview; we will not independently verify
the completeness of what we are given, though we will tell you when something we
expected to see is missing.
Approach
We work in three passes. First, we assemble the arrangement inventory and the
documents behind it, and we reconcile the compensation actually paid against the
compensation the agreements provide for. Second, we develop the fair-market-value
analysis for each arrangement, using published survey data appropriate to the
specialty, geography, and productivity profile, and we document which comparables
we relied on and which we set aside and why. Third, we walk you and your counsel
through findings before anything is final, so that factual corrections happen while
corrections are still cheap.
You will receive a written report at the end. Findings will lead it. Recommendations,
if we have any, will appear in a separate section and will be labeled as
recommendations, so that no one reading the report later can quote a suggestion of
ours as though it were a finding of ours.
The Team
Daniel Alvarado, Senior Consultant, will lead the engagement day to day and will
own the fair-market-value analysis and the report. Daniel does this work constantly
and is the person I send when counsel will be reading closely. Patricia Moore,
Analyst, will handle the data reconciliation and the arrangement inventory, and will
be your team's contact for document requests. I will serve as engagement partner. I
read every deliverable before it leaves this office, and I am available to you directly
at any point in the engagement, including on questions you would rather not put in
writing to the team.
Fees and Payment
Our fee for the scope described above is $114,500, inclusive of out-of-pocket costs
other than travel, which we will bill at actual cost with your prior approval. We will
invoice monthly in arrears based on progress, and invoices are payable within thirty
days of receipt. If the scope changes materially, whether because the arrangement
inventory turns out larger than described or because you ask us to extend into an
area not contemplated here, we will stop, tell you what the change means for fee
Verdant Health Advisory LLC
Page 2 of 4

and schedule, and proceed only on your written agreement. We do not accumulate
out-of-scope time and present it to you afterward.
Term and Termination
The engagement begins on the start date noted above and continues until we deliver
the final report or until terminated as provided here. Either of us may terminate on
ten days' written notice to the other. If you terminate, you will owe fees for work
performed through the termination date, and we will deliver our work product in
whatever state it then stands, with a written note of what remained unfinished so
that no one mistakes a draft for a conclusion. We may also withdraw if we determine
that continuing would place us in a position inconsistent with our professional
obligations, and we would tell you why.
Confidentiality
We will hold in confidence all non-public information you provide, will use it only for
this engagement, and will restrict access to personnel who need it for the work.
Where the information you provide includes protected health information, we will
handle it consistent with your policies and applicable law, and we will ask you to
limit what you send us to the minimum necessary for the analysis; we would rather
receive de-identified productivity data than a file that carries patient identifiers we
have no use for. We will not disclose your confidential information to any third party
except with your consent or as compelled by law or regulatory authority, and in that
event we will notify you promptly unless notification is itself prohibited. Our working
papers remain our property. We will retain them for our standard retention period
and then destroy them.
Limitation of Liability
Our aggregate liability to you arising out of or relating to this engagement, on any
theory, is limited to the fees actually paid to us under this letter. Neither of us will
be liable to the other for indirect, incidental, consequential, special, or punitive
damages, or for lost profits, whether or not the possibility of such damages was
known. This limitation does not apply to liability arising from our fraud or willful
misconduct. Any claim must be brought within one year after the date of the final
deliverable to which it relates.
Our report is prepared for your internal use and for your counsel. It is not to be
quoted, referred to, or distributed to any third party, in whole or in part, without our
prior written consent, and we assume no duty to any third party who obtains it. If a
Verdant Health Advisory LLC
Page 3 of 4

regulator or other authority requests it, tell us and we will cooperate with you on
the response.
Governing Law
This letter is governed by the laws of the state in which our principal office is
located, without regard to its conflict-of-laws provisions. If any provision of this
letter is held unenforceable, the remainder stays in force. This letter, together with
any written scope change signed by both of us, is the entire agreement between us
on this subject and supersedes any prior discussion or proposal.
Acceptance
If the foregoing is acceptable, please sign below and return one copy to my
attention. A signed copy is what starts the clock on our side, and Patricia Moore will
send the initial document request the same day we receive it. Should anything here
need adjustment before you sign, call me rather than marking up the letter; it is
faster, and I would rather hear the objection than read it.
We appreciate the opportunity to work with Johnson, Cross and Gibbs on this review,
and we take seriously that you are bringing us a question with real consequences
attached to the answer.
Very truly yours,
Laura Brown
Managing Partner
Date: May 31, 2021
Laura Phillips
Chief Financial Officer, Johnson, Cross and Gibbs
Date: May 31, 2021
Verdant Health Advisory LLC
Page 4 of 4
